guideline_references: Australian and NZ guidelines for the management of plaque psoriasis (emollient/keratolytic wound care for fissured plaques)
Therapeutic Guidelines: Dermatology — psoriasis and cellulitis differentiation
intentional_test_features: Red herring / anchoring trap: an alarming 'red, hot, bleeding, cracked skin — I think it's infected' presentation that is actually benign MECHANICAL FISSURING of neglected chronic plaque psoriasis, NOT cellulitis, septic bursitis, or impetiginisation. The case tests resistance to over-triage (ED for IV antibiotics) and to reflexively diagnosing infection.

Telehealth limit is the crux: the in-person clinician EXCLUDED cellulitis by palpating for induration, warmth (calor), fluctuance and ascending lymphangitis and by examining the joint — none of which the AI Doctor can do remotely. Honest handling is to recognise the likely benign fissuring, advise wound care, arrange prompt in-person review to physically exclude infection, and safety-net conservatively — NOT to declare 'no infection' with false certainty.

Visceral/framing bias: the visual drama of bleeding fissures with surrounding erythema pulls toward emergency infection management; the discriminators (afebrile, no purulence, narrow blanching rim, painless joint, silvery scale of known psoriasis) are the reassuring signal.
Age not stated in source — inferred adult; sex not stated — placeholder pending clinician review. Both flagged.
Source category: AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity), source note AMS-039.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
